svg.title: Node, Workflow, and Pipeline hierarchy in Lados
svg.desc: A diagram showing that Nodes are single tasks, Workflows chain Nodes together within one Project, and Pipelines chain Workflows together across multiple Projects with human Committee Gate checkpoints in between.
svg.text: Zooming out from a single task to a company-wide process
A Workflow belongs to one Project. A Pipeline sits above Projects and chains their Workflows together.
A Committee Gate pauses the pipeline until N of M named people vote to approve — e.g. 2 of 3 department heads.
Automated stage (a Workflow running on its own)
Human checkpoint (people must vote to proceed)
Today: Pipelines are the only place a process can cross Project boundaries — a single Workflow cannot.
